entry.title: Solution Order Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition kicks off 2025 with a new era of business-empowering, productivity-enhancing and process optimizing innovation – to hear about some of the highlights across the solution, check this video out. As a key part of the Public Cloud ERP, Solution Order Management again delivers new capabilities that help drive increased revenues and growth with optimized solution-based business processes. In this blog, we’ll take a look at the new features being delivered with the 2502 release.

For a brief summary of my top two highlights, take a look at the below video:


Flexible Pricing for Solution Bundles (6BU)

Obtaining higher revenues and growth with solution-based business models results from the ability to combine a range of different types of products in a solution which can be customized and adapted to suit customer needs. A larger scope of more varied offerings could require a high degree of manual input and effort in creating solution orders, so in 2024, we rolled out Advanced Solution Bundling functionality. In a nutshell, solution bundles are pre-configured products consisting of multiple components  packaged together and sold as part of a solution order. To make selling complex & customizable solutions even more efficient, solution bundles are pre-defined and configured in the product master data and can subsequently be added to a solution order, saving solution order specialists time and effort in bundling items. Find out more in this blog post. Revisiting solution bundles in 2502, we have updated them to enable the inclusion of one-time service products which previously weren’t supported with the initial scope.

Notably, with 2502 we have enhanced pricing capabilities to provide greater flexibility to sell items with bundle-specific pricing. Firstly, we have introduced two new price conditions for assigning discounts to solution orders at header level. These define an overall percentage discount based on either the gross amount (price condition type DRG1) or Net amount (price condition type DRN1) of the solution order. The system automatically applies this discount item level, and the discount is applied to all items included in a solution order bundle.

Additionally, we have introduced dedicated pricing for sub-item components within a bundle, increasing flexibility when selling products as part of a bundle or as part of a sales bill of material (BOM) within a bundle. Reflecting whether an item is included in a bundle, this new capability enables you to sell an item at a specific price instead of the standard product price. To support this, new price condition types are now available for solution orders, which can be configured and adjusted in the Manage Prices app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud:

  • PSBP (Bundle Price) – For bundle sub-items that are charged as a one-time fee, such as sales and service items.
  • PSMB (Monthly Bundle Price) – For bundle sub-items that are billed periodically, such as service contract items.
  • PSSK (Sales BOM Price) – For sub-items that are part of a sales BOM within a solution bundle.

When an item is added to a solution order as part of a bundle or sales kit, the system automatically determines its price, and the corresponding price condition type is displayed under the price details tab in the item view. Once the solution order is released, the pricing details are carried forward to all follow-up transactions, ensuring a seamless process and reducing manual effort and input errors when handling complex solution bundles with varied pricing structures.

This feature provides greater flexibility in defining pricing strategies for solution selling and enables businesses to offer a broader and more differentiated portfolio of solution orders, ultimately helping to improve business margins. To ensure the new price condition types are available, make sure to update your reference content to the latest version.

Take an in-depth look at the new pricing parameters for solution bundles in the below demo.



Subscription Billing Enhancements (4GT)

Next, we’ll explore the integration between Solution Order Management and SAP Subscription Billing, where we’ve introduced several key enhancements with the 2502 release. These updates are designed to improve the efficiency of creating, modifying, and managing subscription products within solution orders.

With this release, additional fields have been introduced in Solution Order Management to provide greater flexibility and control over subscription-based offerings. When opening the details view of a subscription billing item in a solution order, you will now see a new checkbox named "Create Subscription as Pending." This feature allows the solution order specialist to control the exact timing of subscription activation. If the checkbox is left unchecked, the original activation setting from SAP Subscription Billing will remain in effect, ensuring seamless integration with the existing configuration. However, if the checkbox is selected, the specialist can override the default setting, providing more control over subscription lifecycle management.

Additionally, a new header section for "Subscription Terms" has been introduced. This section enables users to adapt and modify the default subscription terms defined in SAP Subscription Billing directly within Solution Order Management. By doing so, businesses can manage subscription conditions more effectively and govern the entire lifecycle of follow-up subscriptions without requiring separate modifications in the billing system.

 New fields have also been introduced within the billing section, to further enhance flexibility in subscription sales. These include:

  • Billing Cycle and Billing Cycle Reference Date, which help determine how and when the system schedules billing, allowing for better alignment with business requirements.
  • Additional fields that provide the option to define whether recurring charges should be billed together or waived, giving businesses more options when structuring subscription pricing models.

 Several further fields that were previously only available in SAP Subscription Billing are now reflected in Solution Order Management. These include:

  • Withdraw Period End Date
  • Minimum Term End Date
  • Expected Term End Date
  • Expected Term Extension
  • Subscription Pricing Date
  • Waiver Period Number
  • Waiver Period Number Unit

These fields will be available across multiple touchpoints, including the UI, API, and, where applicable, in output management and the migration object of the Solution Order.

Additionally, the UI of the Subscription Item has been redesigned to provide a more intuitive user experience. Enhancements include:

  • Two new assignment groups for terms and billing information, allowing for a more structured and logical organization of subscription data.
  • A more meaningful assignment of both existing and new subscription fields to their respective assignment groups.
  • Improved presentation of negative charges, making it easier to identify and interpret these amounts.
  • Better visualization of recurring prices, ensuring greater clarity in pricing details.

Finally, to round out these improvements, we have added a new column in the Price Calculation View table called "Recurrence." This column indicates the frequency at which recurring charges are billed, making it easier for users to track and manage subscription-based pricing directly from the solution order interface.

With these enhancements, Solution Order Management now offers greater flexibility and control over subscription-based sales, improving efficiency and reducing manual effort when managing complex billing structures. The improved UI and expanded field availability ensure a more streamlined and user-friendly approach to subscription pricing and lifecycle management.

Click the video below to watch an overview of the new Subscription Billing features delivered with 2502:

 New API v4 for Solution Orders(4GT)


SAP S/4HANA Cloud has taken a major step forward in solution order management with the introduction of a new OData V4-based API. This enhancement brings improved performance, efficiency, and integration capabilities, making it easier than ever to manage solution orders effectively.

One of the biggest advantages of this new API is its optimized communication efficiency. By leveraging OData V4, it reduces data transfer payloads and enhances request handling, resulting in faster processing times and lower resource consumption. This translates to quicker response times and a more streamlined user experience.

Additionally, the API supports full CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) functionality, allowing users to efficiently manage solution order items. While individual items within an order can be modified or removed, solution orders themselves remain protected from deletion, ensuring data integrity and maintaining order history.



Contract Accounts in Solution Orders (4GT)

A key new feature introduced in the 2502 release is the support for FI-CA contract accounts in Solution Orders. FI-CA (Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud is a specialized subledger designed for high-volume billing, invoicing, and receivables management.

With the integration of contract accounts into solution orders, the system retrieves contract account details from the defined payer role and posts the billing information to the FI-CA ledger for further processing. When a payer has a contract account assigned, the system automatically selects it; however, if multiple contract accounts exist for the payer, a popup screen allows users to choose the relevant account from a list.

Contract accounts are maintained both at the header and item levels of the solution order, supporting sales and subscription items. In CFD1 release 1 of 2502, this scope will expand to include service contract items. Notably, different contract accounts can be defined at the header and item levels, allowing for different contract accounts for each solution in cases where multiple solutions are sold in a single order.

When the solution order is released, the specified contract account information is transferred to follow-up documents, which are then processed for billing. Once invoicing is completed, the document is posted to the FI-CA subledger for accounts receivable and payable. From there, users can leverage central contract accounting functions for payment handling, collection management, or clearing open items. The integration of contract accounts into solution orders in SAP S/4HANA Cloud enhances financial management by enabling precise billing, streamlined payments, and improved oversight through the FI-CA component.

This enhancement centralizes all financial transactions related to solution orders under a single contract account, simplifying tracking and reporting. It also automates billing, improving accuracy, reducing manual effort, and helping prevent invoicing discrepancies. Additionally, supporting contract accounts provides a comprehensive view of customer transactions, speeding up the resolution of any billing or payment queries.

Feedback of Reference Equipment for Service Order Items (4GT)

A central use case of solution order is selling related products under a single order efficiently and with minimal manual input. Supporting this, in 2024 we introduced equipment linkage for service contract items to be associated with a physical good in a solution order. With 2502, we expand this functionality to support non-recurring service items to be linked.

At its core, equipment linkage ensures that when the post goods issue is processed for a sales item, the equipment ID and its corresponding serial number are automatically transferred to the associated service. This is particularly beneficial for serialized physical goods, where every unit has a dedicated equipment ID linked to it during the post goods issue. This association is achieved through Reference Objects, which act as a bridge connecting sales items to their respective services. This automation not only reduces errors but also enhances operational efficiency, ensuring that service activities are well-aligned with the relevant product equipment data.

The link between items is created when you establish an item relationship of "One-Time Service for" or "One-Time Service by" between the sales item and the service item in the solution order. This relationship facilitates the effortless transfer of the equipment number, along with its serial number, from the sales item to the service item. It ensures that all related services are comprehensively equipped to proceed.

The below screenshot shows the item relationship being maintained for a one time service in Solution Order Management. The entire process mirrors the process for equipment linkage for service contracts, which you can see here.

The Future of Business, Today: Get Your Access to AI-Assisted Features Thanks to this Limited Promotion Now!  

The 2502 update is a strategic investment that combines advanced AI with powerful enterprise management tools. Whether scaling your business, enhancing productivity, or building sustainable operations, its capabilities help support a more agile and resilient future. 

Sign up for following features through the limited Promotion for AI-assisted Features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (for all countries, customers, and partners): 

  • The easy filter and smart summarization features in SAP Fiori allow for efficient, natural language filtering of business data and seamless summarization capabilities, enabling users to quickly extract relevant information and jump-start subsequent collaboration.  
  • Facilitate easier understanding and interpretation of financial data with insights from cost center reviews and explanations of fixed-asset depreciation keys. 

 

 

More Information 

To catch up with the full scope of Solution Order Management features from previous releases, plus further deep-dive content on Solution Business related topics, check out my collection blog.

 

Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!    

Between January 21 and January 24, we conducted a series of 21 engaging live sessions to showcase the thrilling innovations included in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. If you missed the live sessions, don't worry! You can still access the whole series or select the sessions that interest you the most. Seize this opportunity to watch them on demand at your convenience – anywhere, anytime.   

   

 

Find All of Our Assets    

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Passing Flag Variables to Adobe Form for Conditional Field Visibility
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I was assigned a task to make two fields in an Adobe Form appear or disappear based on a condition specified in the form’s code. I am passing two flag-type variables to the form, and when one of them is true, one field should appear while the other remains hidden, and vice versa.

I’m wondering if there’s a way to pass these flag values directly from the code to the form? I’ve tried several approaches but haven’t been able to get it to work. We also attempted to validate the condition directly in the form’s JavaScript, but without success.

Currently, the solution we’ve implemented involves adding the flag fields to the form and hiding them. This way, they don’t appear in the form, but we can still access the values stored in the variables.

Thanks in advance 🙂.

entry.title: Re: Passing Flag Variables to Adobe Form for Conditional Field Visibility
entry.content: Another option is handle the conditions in the form context which would effectively allow the target fields to be null when appropriate

entry.title: Problems trying to use set_suppress_empty_data
entry.content:
Hi all:

(Running ABAP 7.50)

I’m trying to use the set_suppress_empty_data( abap_true ), but keep getting a dump and an error message (Class DISPLAY_SETTINGS, method SET_SUPPRESS_EMPTY_DATA not supported for CL_SALV_TABLE Control Technology).

My test code is as follows:

REPORT ztest.

DATA alv             TYPE REF TO cl_salv_table.
DATA display_setting TYPE REF TO cl_salv_display_settings.
" ====================================
" Get some sample data
" ====================================
select * FROM trdir
  where name = 'DEMO_EXPRESSIONS'
  into table @DATA(itab).

TRY.
  " ====================================
  " Just a plain CL_SALV_TABLE
  " ====================================
    cl_salv_table=>factory( IMPORTING r_salv_table = alv
                            CHANGING  t_table      = itab ).
    display_setting = alv->get_display_settings( ).
  " ====================================
  " This generates a DUMP (ignores TRY)
  " ====================================
    display_setting->set_suppress_empty_data( abap_true ).

    alv->display( ).
  CATCH cx_salv_msg INTO data(error_cx).
    MESSAGE  error_cx TYPE 'E' DISPLAY LIKE 'I'.
ENDTRY.

The error ignores the TRY…CATCH block.

Any ideas?

TIA,

Luis

entry.title: Teams integrations affected by MC985483 (New default passcode in CreateMeeting API)?
entry.content:
hi!

does anyone know the impact of this announcement of Microsoft  for teams integration?

https://devblogs.microsoft.com/microsoft365dev/announcing-the-deprecation-of-the-ispasscoderequired-property-on-the-microsoft-graph-online-meeting-api/

and https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/answers/questions/2155344/will-the-change-introduced-by-microsoft-in-mc98548

this becomes active in 10 days - February 24 2025? 

 

Could not find any documentation on SAP side?

thank you!

A.

entry.title: Re: SAP Single Sign-On: Protect Your SAP Landscape with X.509 Certificates
entry.content:
I am able to get SSO with X.509 working with already distributed personal x.509 client certificates on our clients. A mandatory step in the setup is that either:
- SAP Cryptolib certificate must be signed by our internal PKI. The Root certificate is already distributed to all clients, so the SLC can validate it.
- Add the self signed SAP Cryptolib certificate of the abap system to the windows store on all clients.
Without this step, we get an "A2200210:Peer certificate verification failed" error as described in note 2680913.
So far, so good.

My curiosity is this:
We have enabled SSO for SAPSUPPORT as described in note "3235573 - Service connection "SAP NI with SNC/SSO". SAP Support personel is able to logon to our Abap system without password and with x.509 certificates generated by SAP's internal PKI.
I would expect that also SAPSupport should get a Peer certificate failed error because you don't have access to our root certificate nor access to the self signed SAP Cryptolib certificate.
But apparently you don't get this error. How is that possible? Does your internal support network bypass some security requirements that apply to normal SSO X.509 scenarios?

entry.title: Public Cloud means continuous innovation and continuous learning – new user group to facilitate both
entry.content:
One of the main benefits of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is that you receive continuous innovations without having to perform upgrades on your systems. Different companies have different ways of managing change, and there are lessons to be learned on the best ways to encourage your business users consume new functionality.

To facilitate sharing of best practices regarding SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition usage, we are hosting an online user group that meets every other month where people can share their best practices and we can demo new innovations, either pre- or post-release. Participants can learn from each other and SAP experts, preview new functionality, provide feedback on the latest features, and participate in user research to shape future features.

The Public Cloud Practitoner Forum meets every other month on the third Thursday at 11am Eastern time / 8am Pacific time / 17:00 CET. We welcome customers from any region, as long as you:

  • are live with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • have 25 or more active users
  • want to empower your users with the best possible experience
  • are open to having your end-users participate in occasional user research projects
  • will attend a 1-hour online meeting every other month

If you would like to join, please ask your account team or sign up here:

https://tinyurl.com/public-cloud-prac-forum

(Invitations will be sent within a week of your signup.)

For the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Practitoner Forum team, Peter Spielvogel and Esther Vrieze.

entry.title: Re: Problems trying to use set_suppress_empty_data
entry.content:
You only catch CX_SALV_MSG and its subclasses, but not CX_SALV_METHOD_NOT_SUPPORTED which is not a subclass of this one but of CX_SALV_NO_CHECK.

  • Syntax of CATCH - A CATCH block handles the exceptions of the exception classes cx_class1 cx_class2 ... that are specified after the statement CATCH, as well as the exceptions of the subclasses of these exception classes
  • The method seems only allowed on list and hierarchy sequence, not tree and grids

entry.title: Re: ways to start as a Consultant
entry.content:
Hi Omar

These are some points I would like to mention on your questions:

Certification Path:

Indeed, you should take start from Associate level certifications and progress to higher level as gaining experience.

  • Take the C_TS4FI certification exam
  • Certification can lead to career opportunities in the SAP field but if you do not have real experience, it's just a certificate.

SAP Financial Consultant Role:

In my opinion you should aim for an SAP Financial consultant once you have necessary skills and experience, first you need to find out the sources where you can get the training, depending on your budget, search online, when you find one, stick with it, as I told that go to SAP Community forums and get the recommendations from SAP FICO consultants, look for a support role even it's a Junior role, you will learn a lot.

Good Luck my friend😉

Regards,

Syed

entry.title: Re: SAP Identity Authentication vs. Local Identity Direct...
entry.content: Hi tskwin, I came here from Google for the same question.

entry.title: Re: SAP Identity Authentication vs. Local Identity Direct...
entry.content:
Hi tskwin, I came here from Google for the same question.

That said, I don't think there is much difference.

The IAS-route requires more configuration and I think it's the old way, the Local Identity Directory is gaining more traction in the documentation since a few months ago. In fact it doesn't even exist in the NEO documentation.

I would argue that LID is the newer way, which supports newer techniques (like application specific groups), paging and central user logs and other improvements that come with the new SCIM v2 API.

From my talks with the CIS-team and the roadmap, I think it's a new direction they're headed in - allowing for better and easier provisioning.

entry.title: Re: Internationalization Formatter and Type Formatter in...
entry.content:
You need to call the MDK clientAPI formatting functions in your rule.  For currency you would call clientAPI.formatCurrency.

context.formatCurrency(1000,'EUR','it-IT','{maximumFractionDigits:2,useGrouping:true}')
"1.000,00 €"

entry.title: Unable to write workflow container in WF Exit
entry.content:
Hey there!

In a workflow definition I added my own multi-line Container that I want to use in a form task. I set the flags IMPORT and EXPORT alternating with always the same effect.

I have added a workflow exit in a task by adding my class in tab programming exits.

This exit will be called several times: before, while and after processing the work item.

at event BEF_EXEC Before Execution I can set values to the wf container with the following code:

 

 

 

DATA(wf_container)         = im_workitem_context->get_wf_container( ).
CASE im_event_name.
  WHEN swrco_event_before_execution.
    lt_mydata = VALUE #( ( field1 = '01' field2 = '02' ) ).
    TRY.
        wf_container->set(
            name       = 'ZMYDATA'
            value      = lt_mydata  ).
      CATCH ...
    ENDTRY.
 ENDCASE.

 

 

 

But: at event AFT_EXEC After Execution I get the exception CX_SWF_CNT_CONT_ACCESS_DENIED

The exception is raised in CL_SWF_CNT_CONTAINER->IF_SWF_CNT_ELEMENT_ACCESS_1~ELEMENT_SET_VALUE_REF which indicates that the container element is only readable and maybe because "element exists locally"?

But why is it possible to set the element value at BEF_EXEC?

 

**** element exists locally.
    IF <element>-props O swfcn_p_read_only.
      cx_element_ro l_elem_ro me name.
      cx_exception_return l_elem_ro.
    ENDIF.

 

Any ideas what I am doing wrong?

Thanks 
~Enno

entry.title: Automatic OP clearing when using an alternative payer
entry.content:
Hello community,

I have the following challenge.
We only use FI in the public cloud. We receive the invoices via interface from a pre-system. The open items and payments are then managed in the public cloud.
We are increasingly working with payment advice notes and automatic clearing of open items. In this scenario, we have the problem that we have different payers. In other words, the customer is the invoice recipient, but the payment advice note and payment come via a different payer. (e.g. central settlement) The open item is for the customer, we have filled the “Alternative payer” field in the business partner with the corresponding payer. We had hoped that when processing the payment advice note, the system would automatically recognize that the alternative payer wants to settle the customer's corresponding open item.
Unfortunately, this does not work.

We have only defined the business partners with the role FLCU00 (customer) and do not use any sales data, and therefore no partner roles in the system.

Does anyone have experience with the “alternative payer” function or with the scenario I have described?
I would be grateful for any ideas.

Thanks in advance for any guidance!

Alex
